title: Eria jerdoniana
intro: Eria jerdoniana, also known as the Indian orchid or E. jerdoniana, is a species of orchid native to the Western Ghats of India. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits, which include sedative, anti-anxiety, and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Eria jerdoniana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Eria jerdoniana include decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health issues, particularly those related to anxiety and sleep disorders.
taxonomy: Eria jerdoniana is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Eria, which belongs to the subtribe Aeridinae. This genus is part of the tribe Vandeae and subfamily Epidendroideae within the family Orchidaceae. Eria jerdoniana is native to the Indian subcontinent, where it is found in various regions. The classification of Eria jerdoniana is further broken down into its binomial nomenclature, which is a scientific name consisting of the genus and species names. The binomial nomenclature of Eria jerdoniana is Eria jerdoniana, which is used universally in the field of botany.
